findings.evidence: `Diagram.lean:70` defines public `def equivPerm`; `StateCardinality.lean:43-64` contains the only `equivPerm_*` simp lemmas.
findings.fix: Move the basic API for `equivPerm` (`equivPerm_apply`, `equivPerm_symm_apply`, and the evaluation/point-set simp lemmas as appropriate) next to the definition in `Diagram.lean`, or keep the equivalence private if it is only an implementation detail for cardinality.
findings.issue: `GridState.equivPerm` is now public in `Diagram.lean`, but its simp characterization lemmas live only in `StateCardinality.lean`, so importing the defining module exposes an uncharacterized equivalence unless users unfold it.

findings.evidence: `StateCardinality.lean:94-106` has `@[reducible] def uniqueOfLeOne ...` with default `(equivPerm n).symm 1`.
findings.fix: Remove `@[reducible]`; add explicit lemmas for any default computation that downstream code genuinely needs instead of relying on unfolding the body.
findings.issue: `uniqueOfLeOne` is marked `@[reducible]`, exposing the construction of the `Unique` instance and its chosen default even though the public API only needs the uniqueness fact/instances.

summary: The cardinality statements are present, but the public surface exposes definitions without keeping their characteristic API at the point of exposure, and one helper unnecessarily exposes its body.
